# Runtime object gap analysis V2

## Executive summary

- All three databases were exported read-only into the same taxonomy and row model.
- No database was modified. No schema patch was generated.
- MSSQL rows: **10987**; bootstrap PG rows: **8110**; runtime PG rows: **9729**.
- Remaining `EXPORT_MISSING`: **0**.
- Remaining `UNKNOWN`: **1865**.

| Object kind | MSSQL | Bootstrap PG | Runtime PG |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| SCHEMA | 1 | 1 | 1 |
| TABLE | 670 | 462 | 670 |
| COLUMN | 5927 | 3839 | 5927 |
| DEFAULT | 695 | 717 | 1163 |
| PRIMARY_KEY | 527 | 359 | 526 |
| FOREIGN_KEY | 13 | 10 | 13 |
| UNIQUE_CONSTRAINT | 5 | 3 | 0 |
| CHECK_CONSTRAINT | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| INDEX | 618 | 377 | 605 |
| TRIGGER | 7 | 1 | 0 |
| VIEW | 8 | 86 | 4 |
| FUNCTION | 120 | 260 | 0 |
| PROCEDURE | 2396 | 1650 | 351 |
| TYPE | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| SEQUENCE | 0 | 345 | 469 |

None. Export coverage is symmetric for the requested taxonomy.

## Conclusion

Exporter coverage is now symmetric for the requested taxonomy, so `EXPORT_MISSING` is no longer used as a placeholder for unqueried PG catalogs.
The diff is sufficiently reliable to prioritize converter/export rules by object kind, but **not yet sufficient to rebuild the runtime database**:
normalized signatures still require reviewed MSSQL→PostgreSQL type/default/expression mappings, and `UNKNOWN` PG-only objects require ownership/source classification.
No production-safety or business-equivalence claim is made.
